text:
## Portal Rough Copy Audit -- Complete Research

### 1. TECH STACK ANSWERS

| Question | Answer |
|----------|--------|
| **Next.js version** | `16.1.6` (latest, uses App Router) |
| **Router** | **App Router** (`app/` directory, `page.tsx` files, `layout.tsx`, server components by default) |
| **UI framework** | **Tailwind CSS v3.4.17** + **shadcn/ui** (default style, neutral base color, CSS variables, `components.json` configured) |
| **Icon library** | **Lucide React** (50+ icons imported across components) |
| **SWR setup** | `swr@^2.4.0` -- used only in `ProposalProvider` to fetch from `/api/proposal`. Basic config: `revalidateOnFocus: false`. Optimistic mutations via `mutate(updated, false)` for field updates, addon toggles, and staffing changes |
| **AI SDK** | `ai@^6.0.73` + `@ai-sdk/react@^3.0.75` -- uses `useChat` hook with `DefaultChatTransport` for streaming. Two chat routes: proposal mixologist (`/api/chat`) and menu assistant (`/api/menu/chat`). Both use `streamText` with model string `"openai/gpt-4o-mini"` (NOT an actual import of the OpenAI SDK) |
| **Real Supabase?** | **NO. All mock data.** API routes use in-memory objects (`let storedProposal = {...}`, `let selectedCocktails = new Set(...)`). No database client anywhere. |
| **Origin** | **v0.dev generated** (`.gitignore` has v0 runtime files, `components.json` uses shadcn schema) |
| **Fonts** | Inter (sans) + Playfair Display (serif) via `next/font/google` |
| **Theme** | Dark mode default via `next-themes`, custom warm gold/amber palette (`--primary: 36 72% 48%`) |

---

### 2. FILE-BY-FILE AUDIT

#### Config Files (KEEP AS-IS or adapt)
| File | Purpose | Verdict |
|------|---------|---------|
| `package.json` | Next 16, shadcn deps, ai SDK, SWR | **Strip** -- portal will be under main app or separate Vercel deploy. Cherry-pick deps. |
| `tailwind.config.ts` | shadcn theme with gold/amber palette, sidebar vars, animations | **KEEP the color palette** -- warm gold `hsl(36, 72%, 48%)` is the brand primary. Port CSS variables to portal. |
| `components.json` | shadcn component registry config | **KEEP** if deploying as standalone Next.js |
| `next.config.mjs` | `ignoreBuildErrors: true`, `unoptimized` images | **Rebuild** -- remove `ignoreBuildErrors` |
| `tsconfig.json` | Standard Next.js + path aliases `@/*` | **KEEP** pattern |

#### App Pages (3 pages -- KEEP ALL, rebuild data layer)
| File | Purpose | Verdict |
|------|---------|---------|
| `app/page.tsx` | **Proposal page** -- main landing. Assembles HeroCard, StatCards, PackageCard, StaffingCard, AddOnsCard, TotalCard, TrustFooter, MixologistChat. Calculates grand total from package + staffing + addons. | **KEEP UI**, replace mock data with Supabase fetch |
| `app/menu/page.tsx` | **Cocktail menu** -- grid of cocktail cards, sticky filter pills, category sections (signature/classic/syrups), floating selection counter, AI chat drawer. Max 4 cocktail selections. | **KEEP UI**, replace mock with Supabase cocktails table |
| `app/contract/page.tsx` | **Contract/agreement** -- 21 collapsible sections, bilingual, e-signature block (name/email/title), PDF download via `window.print()`, expand/collapse all. | **KEEP** -- this is production-quality legal content |
| `app/layout.tsx` | Root layout with Inter + Playfair fonts, ThemeProvider | **KEEP** |
| `app/globals.css` | Custom warm color palette + extensive print/PDF styles | **KEEP** -- the print styles are valuable |

#### API Routes (ALL NEED REBUILDING)
| File | Purpose | Verdict |
|------|---------|---------|
| `app/api/proposal/route.ts` | GET returns mock ProposalData, PATCH updates in-memory fields | **REBUILD** -- replace with Supabase `quotes` table fetch by ID |
| `app/api/chat/route.ts` | Mixologist AI chat using `streamText` with `"openai/gpt-4o-mini"`. System prompt has hardcoded pricing ($45/person, etc). | **REBUILD** -- route to Claude via server proxy, inject real proposal data into system prompt |
| `app/api/menu/route.ts` | GET returns hardcoded cocktail categories, POST toggles selections in-memory Set | **REBUILD** -- connect to Supabase cocktails/selections |
| `app/api/menu/chat/route.ts` | Menu-specific AI chat assistant | **REBUILD** -- same approach as proposal chat |

#### Proposal Components (HIGH VALUE -- KEEP ALL UI)
| File | Lines | Purpose | Verdict |
|------|-------|---------|---------|
| `proposal-provider.tsx` | 112 | SWR data provider + optimistic mutations for field updates, addon toggles, staffing changes | **REBUILD data layer**, keep context pattern |
| `hero-card.tsx` | 70 | Background image hero with editable client name, headline, tagline | **KEEP** -- beautiful design |
| `stat-cards.tsx` | 141 | 2-column grid of editable event detail fields (date, time, guests, location, etc) with empty/filled states | **KEEP** -- sophisticated UX |
| `editable-field.tsx` | 179 | Inline edit component with edit/save/cancel, empty placeholder button, "Unknown" option, user-provided indicator dot | **KEEP** -- this is the crown jewel for interactive proposals |
| `package-card.tsx` | 86 | Package name, per-person price, subtotal, inclusions with checkmarks | **KEEP** |
| `staffing-card.tsx` | 180 | Mixologist count, rate, service hours (editable +/- buttons), setup/teardown, subtotal calculator | **KEEP** |
| `add-ons-card.tsx` | 109 | Toggle switches for add-ons with icons, prices, bilingual names | **KEEP** |
| `total-card.tsx` | 109 | Line items breakdown, grand total, deposit %, "Accept & Book" CTA button (disabled until times filled) | **KEEP** |
| `trust-footer.tsx` | 78 | Contact info, SMS/website/Instagram links, quote validity, logo | **KEEP** |
| `portal-tabs.tsx` | 40 | 3-tab nav (Proposal / Menu / Contract) using Next.js Link + pathname | **KEEP** |
| `proposal-progress.tsx` | 94 | Progress bar + field status grid showing 9 required fields | **KEEP** |
| `completion-banner.tsx` | 77 | Banner showing missing field count with pill badges | **KEEP** |
| `language-provider.tsx` | 33 | React context for EN/FR locale switching | **KEEP** |
| `language-toggle.tsx` | 36 | EN/FR toggle button pair | **KEEP** |
| `theme-provider.tsx` | 12 | next-themes wrapper, dark mode default | **KEEP** |
| `theme-toggle.tsx` | 37 | Sun/moon toggle for light/dark | **KEEP** |
| `mixologist-chat.tsx` | 172 | Floating chat bubble + panel with AI chat via `useChat`. Wine icon, welcome message, streaming indicator. | **KEEP UI**, rebuild transport |

#### Menu Components
| File | Lines | Purpose | Verdict |
|------|-------|---------|---------|
| `cocktail-card.tsx` | 142 | Image card with selection state, mocktail badge, spirit/style tags, price, syrup link | **KEEP** -- beautiful card design |

#### Lib Files
| File | Purpose | Verdict |
|------|---------|---------|
| `lib/proposal-types.ts` | `ProposalData`, `ProposalField`, `AddOnData` interfaces + completeness checker | **ADAPT** -- map to main app's Quote type |
| `lib/translations.ts` | 500+ line bilingual string file (EN + FR) covering all 3 tabs | **KEEP** -- massive value, includes full contract text in both languages |
| `lib/utils.ts` | `cn()` helper (clsx + tailwind-merge) | **KEEP** |

#### UI Components (shadcn)
42 shadcn/ui primitives in `components/ui/`. Standard library components. **No need to copy** -- install fresh via `npx shadcn@latest add`.

---

### 3. SCHEMA MAPPING: ProposalData vs Quote

**Rough Copy: `ProposalData`** (flat structure with `ProposalField` wrappers)
```
ProposalField = { value: string | number | null, source: "backend" | "user" | null }
```

| ProposalData field | Type | Quote equivalent | Notes |
|-------------------|------|------------------|-------|
| `id` | string | `Quote.id` | Direct map |
| `clientName` | ProposalField | `Quote.data.clientName` | Unwrap from string to ProposalField |
| `clientEmail` | ProposalField | `Quote.client_email` + `Quote.data.clientEmail` | Both exist |
| `clientPhone` | ProposalField | `Quote.data.clientPhone` | Optional in Quote |
| `eventDate` | ProposalField | `Quote.event_date` + `Quote.data.eventDate` | Both exist |
| `startTime` | ProposalField | `Quote.data.eventTime` | **Name mismatch**: startTime vs eventTime |
| `endTime` | ProposalField | `Quote.data.endTime` | Direct map |
| `guests` | ProposalField | `Quote.data.headcount` | **Name mismatch**: guests vs headcount |
| `location` | ProposalField | `Quote.data.location` | Direct map |
| `address` | ProposalField | -- | **MISSING in Quote** -- need to add or store in `data` JSONB |
| `packageName` | string | Derived from `EventTypeConfig.packageName` | Config-driven in main app |
| `packageDescription` | string | Derived from `EventTypeConfig.subhead` | Config-driven |
| `pricePerPerson` | number | `Quote.data.subtotal / headcount` | Calculated |
| `inclusions` | string[] | `Quote.data.lineItems` (non-optional) | Array of QuoteLineItem in main app |
| `depositPercent` | number | Hardcoded 25% in Quote | Need to parameterize |
| `contactName` | string | Hardcoded "Ashley" | Static config |
| `companyPhone` | string | Hardcoded | Static config |
| `validDays` | number | Derived from `Quote.expires_at` | Calculate from expiry |
| `addOns[]` | AddOnData[] | `Quote.data.selectedAddons` + `addons` table | Main app has proper addons table |
| `staffing` | object | -- | **MISSING in Quote** -- need staffing fields in JSONB or new columns |

**Key gaps to fill:**
1. `address` field -- not in Quote, add to `data` JSONB
2. `staffing` object (ratePerHour, mixologistsRequired, serviceHours, setupHours, teardownHours) -- not in Quote, add to `data` JSONB
3. `ProposalField` wrapper pattern (value + source tracking) -- doesn't exist in Quote. Add `client_edits` JSONB column to track which fields the client modified
4. `startTime` vs `eventTime` naming -- rough copy is better (separate start/end)
5. `cocktailSelections` exists in Quote but has no detail -- needs to join with a cocktails reference table

**Recommended Quote `data` JSONB additions:**
```ts
{
  ...existing QuoteData fields,
  address?: string;
  staffing: {
    ratePerHour: number;
    mixologistsRequired: number;
    serviceHours: number;
    setupHours: number;
    teardownHours: number;
  };
  clientEdits: Record<string, { value: string; editedAt: string }>;
  contractSigned?: boolean;
  contractSignedBy?: string;
  contractSignedAt?: string;
}
```

---

### 4. WHAT'S PRODUCTION-READY vs NEEDS WORK

**Production-Ready (just works):**
- All UI components (excellent design, accessibility, responsive)
- Bilingual content system (EN/FR with 250+ translation keys)
- Contract page with all 21 legal sections
- Print/PDF CSS styles
- Dark/light theme with warm brand palette
- EditableField inline editing UX
- CocktailCard visual design
- Tab navigation pattern

**Needs Rebuilding:**
- All 4 API routes (mock data -> Supabase)
- ProposalProvider data fetching (SWR -> Supabase client or fetch with auth token)
- Chat transport (generic AI SDK -> Claude via server proxy)
- Menu data (hardcoded -> Supabase cocktails table)
- Contract signature persistence (fetch -> Supabase)
- Authentication/authorization (none -> quote token-based access)

**Missing Features to Add:**
- Quote token-based URL access (`/portal/[token]`)
- View tracking (the `quote_views` table already exists)
- Real Stripe checkout integration (CTA button -> payment link)
- Contract signature -> Supabase persistence
- Real cocktail menu from database
- Cocktail selection persistence per quote

---

### 5. DEPENDENCY ANALYSIS

**Keep from rough copy:**
- `next-themes` (already compatible pattern)
- `lucide-react` (icon library -- main app already uses it)
- `swr` (good for portal data fetching; alternative: direct Supabase realtime)
- `tailwind-merge` + `clsx` (via `cn()`)
- `tailwindcss-animate` (for accordion animations)

**Drop:**
- `ai` + `@ai-sdk/react` -- chat will use our existing Claude server proxy
- `react-hook-form` + `@hookform/resolvers` + `zod` -- not used anywhere in code
- `recharts` -- not used
- All 30+ Radix primitives -- install only what's needed via shadcn CLI
- `embla-carousel-react`, `react-resizable-panels`, `vaul` -- not used
- `sonner`, `cmdk`, `input-otp` -- not used

**Actual shadcn components used:**
Only `tabs` is imported from the UI directory. The rest of the UI is custom Tailwind. The 42 shadcn primitives were auto-generated by v0 but mostly unused.

---

### 6. ARCHITECTURE RECOMMENDATION

The rough copy should be deployed as a **separate Next.js app on Vercel** (not embedded in the main Vite app). Reasoning:
1. It's a client-facing portal -- different audience than the owner CRM
2. Next.js App Router gives SSR/SEO for the portal pages
3. API routes can proxy to Supabase + Claude
4. Independent deploy cycle
5. URL structure: `portal.mtlcraftcocktails.com/[quote-token]`

The main app's `ProposalTab.tsx` and `QuotePage.tsx` can be deprecated once the portal goes live -- they're less polished versions of the same concept.
summary: Complete rough copy audit with schema mapping

text:
Research complete. Here are the full findings:

---

## 1. CURRENT IMPLEMENTATION ANALYSIS

### CalendarWidget.tsx (components/CalendarWidget.tsx)
- **Weekly grid view** (Sun-Sat), 7-column CSS grid layout
- Fetches from Google Calendar via `fetchGoogleCalendarEvents(calendarId, start, end)`
- Data type: `CalendarEvent { id, title, start: Date, end: Date, location?, description?, calendarId? }`
- Features: infinite scroll (loads 4 weeks initially, 2 more on scroll), expandable event cards, voice navigation via `useImperativeHandle` (navigateTo, navigateToNextWeek, findEventByTitle, getEventsForDay)
- Styling: uses old `card-light`, `surface-50`, `ink-*` design tokens (NOT the current slate/cyan system)
- This component appears to be the **standalone calendar page**, separate from the bento grid calendar

### crm-bento-grid.tsx Calendar Section (CalendarSkeleton component, ~line 1197-1490)
- **Month grid view** - generates calendar dynamically based on current month
- Uses `convertEventsToCalendarFormat(events: EventData[])` which maps EventData[] into `{ eventsByDay, upcomingDays }` format
- EventData input: `{ id, eventType, clientName, eventDate, headcount, ... }` (from types.ts)
- Calendar output: `{ day, weekday, month, events: [{ id, title, time, type, guests, originalEvent }] }`
- Already has glass styling: `bg-white/70 backdrop-blur-md` on month grid, `bg-white/80 backdrop-blur-sm` on day cards
- Event dots: colored by type (wedding=rose, corporate=blue, etc.), count badges for 3+ events
- Hover tooltips on days with events (positioned via fixed portal)
- Scrollable daily schedule below the month grid with expandable event details
- Quick add via `+` button on free days or within expanded day cards

### QuickAddEventModal (inline in crm-bento-grid.tsx, ~line 1805-1940)
- Yes, it exists - defined inline, NOT a separate component file
- Fields: Client Name (text input), Date (readonly, prefilled), Time (time input), Event Type (select: Private Party, Wedding, Corporate Event, Workshop)
- Already has glass styling: `bg-white/80 backdrop-blur-xl border-white/20 rounded-3xl`
- Calls `onEventCreated(event)` with: `{ clientName, date, startTime, eventType, status: 'Inquiry' }`

---

## 2. LIBRARY RESEARCH

### macos-calendar (npm: macos-calendar)
- **VERDICT: DO NOT USE**
- 5 stars, 0 forks, last published **6 years ago** (v1.0.5)
- No React version requirements documented - likely class components
- Will NOT work with React 19
- Dead project, no maintenance

### liquid-glass-react (npm: liquid-glass-react)
- **VERDICT: VIABLE for accent glass effects**
- GitHub: rdev/liquid-glass-react - **4.4k stars**, 290 forks, MIT license
- **Requires React >=19.0.0** - perfect match for our React 19.2.0
- TypeScript 95.5%, ESM + CJS dual output, esbuild bundler
- Demo app uses Next.js 15.3.3 + Tailwind v4 (similar stack)
- Should work fine with Vite 6 (standard ESM package)
- **Four refraction modes**: standard, polar, prominent, shader
- Key props: `displacementScale`, `blurAmount`, `saturation`, `aberrationIntensity`, `elasticity`, `cornerRadius`, `padding`, `className`, `overLight`, `onClick`, `mode`
- **CRITICAL LIMITATION**: Safari and Firefox only show partial effects (displacement NOT visible due to SVG filter limitations). Chrome-only for full refraction effect.
- The "shader" mode (WebGL) is less stable

### @liquidglass/react (alternative npm package)
- Separate package using backdrop filters + SVG displacement mapping
- Less popular than rdev's version

### Aceternity UI Pro
- **No glass-specific components**. No calendar components.
- Pro has: Hero Sections, Bento Grids, Cards, Backgrounds, Shaders - none specifically for glassmorphism
- The project already uses "Aceternity-style" patterns implemented with **pure Tailwind** (EventCardHover.tsx uses `bg-white/95 backdrop-blur-md`)

### Other Apple Calendar React Libraries
- **None exist** for React 19. The ecosystem has react-big-calendar, react-day-picker, react-calendar, HeroUI Calendar - but none replicate Apple Calendar's visual style. They're functional, not design-matching.

---

## 3. ANSWERS TO KEY QUESTIONS

**Does macos-calendar work with React 19?** No. Dead project, 6 years stale, likely class components.

**Does liquid-glass-react work with Vite 6?** Yes. Standard ESM package, peer deps are React >=19, no bundler lock-in. Demo uses Tailwind v4.

**What Aceternity Pro components provide glass effects?** None specifically. The project's existing glass effects are pure Tailwind `backdrop-blur-*` + semi-transparent backgrounds, which is the industry standard approach.

**How does the current calendar render events?** Two systems:
1. CalendarWidget.tsx: weekly grid, `CalendarEvent { id, title, start: Date, end: Date }` from Google Calendar API
2. crm-bento-grid CalendarSkeleton: month grid, `EventData` converted via `convertEventsToCalendarFormat()` into `{ day, weekday, month, events: [{ id, title, time, type, guests }] }`

**Does QuickAddEventModal exist?** Yes, inline in crm-bento-grid.tsx. Fields: Client Name, Date (prefilled), Time, Event Type dropdown. Already has glass styling.

---

## 4. RECOMMENDATIONS

### Calendar Component
**Build custom with Tailwind + Framer Motion.** No viable Apple Calendar library exists for React 19. The current `CalendarSkeleton` is already 80% there - it has the month grid, event dots, hover tooltips, and daily schedule. The redesign should enhance this existing component rather than introduce a new library.

### Glass Effects - Two-Tier Approach
1. **liquid-glass-react** for 1-2 hero/accent elements where the full refraction effect adds wow factor (e.g., the calendar card container or the month grid header). The Safari/Firefox limitation is acceptable since this is an owner-facing ops tool (Chrome usage likely).
2. **Tailwind backdrop-filter** for all general glassmorphism (already the established pattern - 14+ instances in crm-bento-grid.tsx). This provides consistent cross-browser glass effects.

### Fallback
If liquid-glass-react adds too much complexity or the Safari limitation is unacceptable: pure Tailwind `backdrop-blur-md` + `bg-white/70` + `border-white/20` achieves 90% of the visual effect and is already proven across the codebase.

### Dependencies to Add
- `liquid-glass-react` (if going with the two-tier approach) - no other new deps needed
- The project already has `framer-motion`, `tailwindcss`, `date-fns` - everything needed for custom calendar work

Research saved to `~/.claude/memory/research/2026-02-07-calendar-liquid-glass-research.md`
summary: Calendar + Liquid Glass research complete with recommendations

text:
Task #3 is already done. I sent you the full research findings in my previous message. Key summary:

- **macos-calendar**: Dead project (6 years old, 5 stars). Do not use.
- **liquid-glass-react**: Viable. 4.4k stars, requires React >=19.0.0 (matches our stack), works with Vite 6. Safari/Firefox partial support only.
- **Aceternity UI Pro**: No glass-specific or calendar components. Project already uses Aceternity-style patterns via pure Tailwind.
- **No Apple Calendar React library exists** for React 19.

**Recommendation**: Build custom calendar with Tailwind + Framer Motion (current CalendarSkeleton is 80% there). Use liquid-glass-react for 1-2 accent elements, Tailwind backdrop-blur for general glass effects.

Research saved to `~/.claude/memory/research/2026-02-07-calendar-liquid-glass-research.md`.

Ready for next task assignment.
summary: Task #3 already completed, research sent earlier

text:
Task #6 was already completed -- I sent you the full audit earlier. I've now also saved the structured findings to `~/.claude/memory/research/2026-02-07-portal-research.md` as requested.

Key highlights from the research:
- Rough copy is v0.dev generated, Next.js 16 App Router, all mock data (no Supabase)
- UI is production-quality (17 components, bilingual, dark/light, print styles)
- All 4 API routes need rebuilding to connect to Supabase
- 5 schema gaps identified between ProposalData and Quote type (address, staffing, field source tracking, naming mismatches)
- Recommend deploying as separate Next.js app on Vercel
- 42 shadcn primitives were auto-generated but only `tabs` is actually used

Ready for next task assignment.
summary: Task 6 already done, memory file saved

text:
CRM Phase 1A (Stories 9 and 10) is complete. Build passes clean.

**Story 9 - Quick Action Enhancements:**
- Added Edit button (Pencil icon) to EventTilesSkeleton hover quick actions alongside Phone/Email/Map
- Added full quick action dock to EventCardHover overlay: Call, Email, Map, Edit buttons
- All buttons use `e.stopPropagation()` to prevent card click-through
- Made hover overlay `pointer-events-auto` when visible so dock buttons are clickable

**Story 10 - Status Dropdown:**
- Added `onUpdateEventStatus` prop to `CRMBentoGridProps` and wired it through to EventTilesSkeleton
- Replaced the TODO console.log in `handleStatusChange` with actual `onUpdateEventStatus?.()` call
- Replaced native `<select>` with custom styled dropdown button + AnimatePresence animated menu
- Each status option shows a colored dot (blue/amber/green/purple/slate) and label
- Dropdown closes on selection and on mouse leave from tile
- Added status dropdown to EventCardHover overlay with same 5 statuses and color dots
- Added `onUpdateStatus` prop to EventCardHoverProps

**Files modified:**
- `components/crm-bento-grid.tsx` - Props, status dropdown, edit button, handler wiring
- `components/EventCardHover.tsx` - Props, quick action dock, status dropdown, pointer-events fix
summary: CRM Phase 1A Stories 9+10 complete, build passes

text:
Task #7 (Portal Phase 1: Wire to Supabase) is complete. Build passes.

**Files created:**
- `lib/supabase.ts` -- Server and browser Supabase clients
- `lib/quote-types.ts` -- Quote/QuoteData types mirroring main app
- `lib/quote-mapper.ts` -- Converts Supabase `Quote` row to `ProposalData` with:
  - Field wrapping (value/source) with clientEdits override support
  - Package config derived from event type
  - Add-on mapping from known IDs with headcount-based pricing
  - Staffing calculation (1 mixologist per 40 guests, $40/hr, 1h setup + 1h teardown)
  - Date formatting
- `app/[token]/page.tsx` -- Dynamic route for clean URLs (`/abc123`)
- `.env.local.example` -- Required env vars documented

**Files modified:**
- `app/api/proposal/route.ts` -- Rewrote GET/PATCH with Supabase:
  - GET: Fetches quote by UUID token, validates format, checks expiry, records view in `quote_views`, updates status to 'viewed' on first access, maps via `mapQuoteToProposal`
  - PATCH: Stores client field edits in `data.clientEdits` JSONB, handles add-on toggles + staffing changes, returns updated ProposalData
- `components/proposal/proposal-provider.tsx` -- Now accepts `token` prop, passes it as query param to API
- `app/page.tsx` -- Supports `?token=` query param fallback, shows landing page if no token

**Security:**
- UUID validation on all token inputs (regex check)
- Service key only used server-side in API routes
- No auth sessions needed -- quote UUID serves as access token
- Expired quotes return HTTP 410

**Installed:** `@supabase/supabase-js` (via npm --legacy-peer-deps due to existing date-fns peer conflict)

Build: `next build` passes clean with all routes correctly typed.
summary: Task 7 complete - Portal wired to Supabase
